title: Trades, signals, straddle, and retrospection REST API routes
acceptance_criteria: src/utils/market-hours.ts adds: toIstDate(utcDate: Date): string (returns YYYY-MM-DD in IST UTC+5:30) and istDayRange(isoDate: string): { from: Date; to: Date } (returns UTC Date objects for IST midnight boundaries of that date)

GET /api/trades Zod schema: { status?: z.enum(['open','closed','stopped']), personality_id?: z.string().uuid(), date?: z.string() validated as YYYY-MM-DD, limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(500).default(100) }; date param parsed via istDayRange() for query bounds

GET /api/signals Zod schema: { underlying?: z.enum(['NIFTY','BANKNIFTY','SENSEX']), limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50), date?: YYYY-MM-DD, min_probability?: z.coerce.number().min(0).max(1) }
GET /api/straddle/latest returns most recent straddle_snapshots row per underlying

GET /api/straddle/history Zod schema: { underlying: z.enum(['NIFTY','BANKNIFTY','SENSEX']), expiry?: z.string(), from?: IST datetime, to?: IST datetime, limit: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(500).default(200) }
GET /api/retrospection Zod schema: { date?: YYYY-MM-DD, personality_id?: uuid }; returns all results for date if no personality_id
Invalid Zod schema → 400 with descriptive error message (not a 500)
All DB queries use parameterized values — no string interpolation of user input
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
